name: Gingerbread Icing and Assembly
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, beat the sugar, meringue powder, and 3/4 cup water on low until blended.
2. Beat on high for 8-10 minutes or until stiff peaks form, adding additional water 1 tablespoon at a time if needed.
3. Cover the bowl tightly with a damp paper towel and set aside until ready to use.
To assemble:
1. Test cookie pieces to ensure they fit together snugly. If necessary, file carefully with a serrated knife or emery board.
2. Fill decorating bag two-thirds full with icing.
3. Begin at the front of the house: squeeze a 3/8 inch wide strip of icing onto the bottom edge of the front piece and position it 3 inches from the front edge of the base. Let the assembly prop upright with spice jars for 2-3 minutes, or until the icing hardens; then remove the jars.
4. Add the sides by squeezing icing on the lower edge of one side piece and the side edge of the front piece. Align pieces at a right angle, ensuring they are as tight as possible. Repeat with the other side.
5. Attach the back: squeeze icing onto the bottom and side edges of the back piece and position it with the assembled front and side pieces. For added stability, squeeze icing along the inside edge of all pieces and corners.
6. Assemble the roof by working on one side at a time:
- Squeeze icing onto the upper edge of the slant of the front and back pieces.
- Also, squeeze icing onto the adjoining side piece.
- Carefully place the roof piece so that its peak is even with the points of the front and back (with an overhang of 1/2 inch).
7. Repeat on the other side of the roof.
8. Add a chimney if desired.
9. Decorate the house with remaining icing, candies, and cookies of your choice.
